REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO THE CLAIM FOR FRAUD IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ASSERT THIS CLAIM AS A SEPARATELY LABELED CAUSE OF ACTION AND TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADING BASED ON ISSUE PRECLUSION IS DENIED. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE FILINGS, INCLUDING THE JUDGMENT IN THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER CASE, CUD-22-668828. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT IN THE UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION THE ISSUES PRESENTED "WERE ACTUALLY LITIGATED AND NECESSARY FOR A DETERMINATION OF THE LAWSUIT." UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTIONS INCLUDE PROCEDURAL AND SUBSTANTIVE COMPONENTS, ALL OF WHICH WERE RAISED IN THE ACTION AND ADDRESSED IN THE JURY INSTRUCTIONS. (E.G. 4305; 4308(4); 4309; 4324; 4325(2)). NOTHING ON THE FACE OF THE JUDGMENT INDICATES WHAT THE BASIS FOR THE JURY VERDICT WAS. THE JUDGMENT MERELY STATES THAT DEFENDANT IS THE PREVAILING PARTY IN AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION. THE SOLE ISSUE ON A CLAIM FOR UNLAWFUL DETAINER IS POSSESSION. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Aug-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Sam Barrager 503-258-7870 plaintiff in pro per appearing via Zoom.
Jessica Alexandra 415-500-6089 of Alexandra Law Firm appearing via Zoom for defendant.

Defendant contests tentative. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings

is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Judgment on the Pleadings as to the claim for fraud is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to assert this claim as a separately labeled cause of action and to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action. Judgment on the Pleading based on issue preclusion is DENIED. The Court takes judicial notice of the filings, including the judgment in the unlawful detainer case, CUD-22-668828. Moving party failed to show that in the unlawful detainer action the issues presented "were actually litigated and necessary for a determination of the lawsuit." Unlawful detainer actions include procedural and substantive components, all of which were raised in the action and addressed in the Jury Instructions. (e.g. 4305; 4308(4); 4309; 4324; 4325(2)). Nothing on the face of the Judgment indicates what the basis for the jury verdict was. The judgment merely states that Defendant is the prevailing party in an unlawful detainer action. The sole issue on a claim for unlawful detainer is possession.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Sam Barrager 503-258-7870 plaintiff in pro per appearing via Zoom.
Jessica Alexandra 415-500-6089 of Alexandra Law Firm appearing via Zoom for defendant.

Plantiff contests attorney's fees only. Discussed. The court adopts the tentative ruling with the only change in the amount of attorney's fees granted. The court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT LAURA DECARAVA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant moves to strike the entire complaint, or alternatively specified paragraphs of the complaint per CCP 425.16.
Motion to strike the entire complaint is DENIED. Moving party fails to address how the entire complaint and each claim contained therein arises out of protected activity.
Motion to Strike specified paragraphs of the complaint upon which each of the asserted claims in the complaint is based is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Each of plaintiff's claims arise out of allegations contained in paragraphs 4-38 as is expressly alleged in the Complaint (see paragraphs 41, 46, 51, 56, 61, 66). Defendant argues that any claim arising out of the rent board petition, police reports and application for a civil harassment is subject to CCP 425.16. Defendant shifted its burden on the first prong of the statute. However, Plaintiff has shown probability of prevailing on the merits as to some of the claims.
The motion to strike is denied (1) as it pertains to the claim for abuse of process re: violation of a restraining order; See Siam v. Kizilbash (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1563; (2) the motion is denied as to all claims arising of false police reports CC 47(b)(5). Plaintiff's Reliance on Hagberg v. Cal. Fed. Bank, decided 17 years before he enactment of CC 47(b)(5) is not supported by any authority. Hagberg v. California Federal Bank (2004) 32 Cal.4th 350.
Motion to strike is granted (1) as to the claim for malicious prosecution as such claim may not be based on a civil harassment action as a matter of law. Siam v. Kizilbash (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 1563; and as to (2) the following paragraphs of the complaint: Paragraph 11 in its entirety; 20 in its entirety; 24 the first and the third sentences only; 28 the second and the third sentence only; 31 in its entirety, 37 in its entirety; 38 the words :denying Defendant's restraining order, and" only.
Attorney's fees granted in the amount of $2,500.

Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
